title: 控方证人
summary:
Jack, Felice, and Lavinton gather in a dark room to conduct a séance involving the blue porcelain jar. Lavinton instructs the group to enter a trance-like state and cast aside fear. During the session, Felice expresses a sense of impending terror, and Jack experiences a suffocating sensation before losing consciousness.

Jack wakes up at 12:30 the following day lying in a shrubbery near a villa. Finding the villa empty, he returns to his hotel, where he encounters his uncle, George. George, having arrived from Dover, is furious when Jack mentions the séance. George explains that the blue porcelain jar is a unique Ming Dynasty piece worth at least £10,000, which he acquired from an American millionaire named Hugenheim. Jack then discovers a note from Lavinton, who identifies himself as "The Soul Doctor." The note states that he, Felice, and her sick father left twelve hours prior, implying that the supernatural experience during the séance was a deception achieved through scientific means.

In a separate narrative, psychologist Edward Castells receives a telegram from his friend Settle regarding the case of Sir Arthur Camichael. Settle explains that Arthur, a twenty-three-year-old man from the upper class, has suddenly transformed into a "half-idiot" who cannot recognize anyone. As Castells and Settle travel toward Walden in a carriage, Settle suggests the issue is related to the house itself and hints at a terrifying, inexplicable presence within it. Castells, who admits to believing in the supernatural despite his profession, agrees to approach the case without prejudice.
